tasks.description: Reduce `PinnedShotGroup` in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts` to soft-tag fields only: `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, `mode`, `videoAssetKey`, `imageClipSnapshot`. Remove `start`, `children`, and any per-child geometry. Add a one-line comment documenting that `clipIds` is ordered left-to-right by live `at` and rebuilt on membership change.

In `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts` (`repairConfig`), detect legacy pinned groups with `start`+`children` and convert to soft-tag shape by deriving `clipIds = children.map(c => c.clipId)` and dropping `start`/`children`. Add a migration unit test that loads a projection-shape config, asserts the converted soft-tag shape, and round-trips via save asserting no `start`/`children` are emitted.

Strip projection from runtime build/save assembly. Update `clonePinnedShotGroups()` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts` to copy only soft-tag fields. Update `rowsToConfig()` to never re-derive `start`/`children` — emit soft-tag shape sourced from current live `clipIds`. Remove the `projectPinnedShotGroups` step from `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts`. Update `src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts:122-159` and any other fixtures asserting the expanded shape.

Move ownership of `interactionStateRef: { drag: boolean; resize: boolean }` from `TimelineEditor.tsx:191-204` up into `useTimelineState.ts:38-44`. Return the ref from `useTimelineState` and pass it to `TimelineEditor`, `useTimelineSave`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` via their hook arguments. Verify `useClipDrag` and `useClipResize` consume the same shared ref.
tasks.executor_notes: Supporting plumbing may require updates to `useTimelineState.types.ts`/`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`/`VideoEditorProvider.tsx` to expose the ref consistently. Keep plumbing minimal.

tasks.description: Delete projection-era helpers in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts`. Keep only `findEnclosingPinnedGroup`, `categorizeSelection`, and a new small `orderClipIdsByAt(clipIds, clips)` helper. Delete `projectPinnedShotGroups`, `resizeGroupEdge`, `resizeChildBoundary`, `replaceGroup`, `replaceGroupChildren`, `buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `normalizeChildren`, `setClipTimelineDuration`, `moveGroup`, `getGroupDuration`. NOTE: `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is a LOCAL helper in `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts` (not a pinned-group-projection export) — it will be removed when T11 rewrites `switchToImages()` inline. Rewrite `pinned-group-projection.test.ts` for surviving helpers. Delete `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` outright (its `isInteractionActive` harness wires into removed helpers).

Delete the in-commit reconciler and its auto-restore behavior in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts`. Delete `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` at :110-255 entirely. Update `materializeData()` at :309-335 to drop the reconciler call and pass `rows`/`meta`/`clipOrder`/`current.config.pinnedShotGroups` directly to `rowsToConfig()`. Drop `getSnapshotDuration` if it becomes dead. DELIBERATE product change: drop the hidden reverse auto-restore at :172-246 entirely — this is ONLY the hidden reconciler path, NOT the explicit user-invocable `switchToImages()` which is preserved in T11. Delete-shot will delete member clips via `rows` and remove the entry in the same commit (no auto-restore). Add a regression test: deleting a video-mode group's video clip leaves (a) clips gone, (b) group preserved with empty `clipIds`, (c) no images auto-inserted.

Rebuild grouped resize as direct clip mutations in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts`. Replace `startGroupEdgeResize`: resolve first/last clip from `group.clipIds` ordered by live `at` and emit `applyEdit({ type: 'rows' })` mutating only that clip's `at`/`hold`/source-time. Replace `resizeChildBoundary`: two-clip mutation where `clip[i].hold` shrinks/grows and `clip[i+1].at` AND `clip[i+1].hold` change so `clip[i+1]`'s right edge stays put. Reuse existing free-clip source-time math. Add tests for first-edge, last-edge, interior-boundary, and a sequence (resize → drag → resize).

Collapse `group-edge` and `group-child-boundary` resize sessions into the regular `clip` session in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`. Extend the `clip` resize session payload (:649-702, :801-837, :858-933) with optional `pairClipId`/`pairBoundary` fields. Compute preview overrides for both clips in the same render pass and apply boundary clamps using the pair. Remove `group-edge`/`group-child-boundary` session branches. Drop `onShotGroupEdgeResize` from the canvas.

Rewrite grouped drag in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` to direct clip mutations. Audit the three caller branches at :408-505. Same-track grouped drag → `type: 'rows'` translating each member by the same delta; re-derive `clipIds` from post-`resolveOverlaps` `at` order; include a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in the same commit if order changed. New-track grouped drag → `type: 'config'` with `nextResolvedConfig` containing the new track and translated clips plus a soft-tag override, in one commit. Constrain `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` typing in `useTimelineCommit.ts` to soft-tag shape on both `rows` and `config` variants. Simplify `src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts` (`PlannedGroupMove`) to per-clip target only. Remove `findBestGroupStart`/`GroupExtent` from `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts` if unused. Extend drag tests with no-collision, colliding, and new-track grouped cases.

Bring `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` onto the soft-tag model. Rewrite `moveClipToRow()` (:88-115, :183-213): existing-track grouped move → single `rows` mutation + soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`. Rewrite `createTrackAndMoveClip()` (:289-427): single `config` commit with new track and soft-tag override. No `moveGroup()` calls remain. Audit keyboard `move-selected` and batch move callers. Update `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`.

Simplify group mutation entry points. (1) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts`, restrict `type: 'pinnedShotGroups'` to membership/metadata only. (2) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`: rewrite BOTH directions. Forward `switchToFinalVideo()` (:81-123) — delete member image clips via `rows`, insert the video clip at the group's leftmost current live `at`, update `clipIds`/`mode`/`videoAssetKey` via soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`, one commit. Reverse `switchToImages()` (:318-412) — EXPLICIT user feature, PRESERVED via soft-tag rewrite: anchor = video clip's live `at` (`videoAction.start` at :334); insert fresh image clips via a `type: 'rows'` commit (the only variant carrying `metaUpdates` + `metaDeletes` + `clipOrderOverride` + `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` together per useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65), computing each clip's `at = anchor + cumulativeOffset` walking `imageClipSnapshot` in order using `getClipDuration(snapshot.meta, videoAction)`; delete the video clip in the same commit (`metaDeletes: [videoClipId]`, `clipOrderOverride` patched, action removed from `nextRows`); update group via soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` (`clipIds = imageClipSnapshot.map(s => s.clipId)`, `mode = 'images'`, `imageClipSnapshot` preserved, `videoAssetKey` cleared). Remove the local `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` helper and its import sites; inline soft-tag construction. No `start`/`children`/`offset` reads anywhere. (3) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:112-166` (pin/unpin/sync), use soft-tag fields only. Pin sorts by live `at`; unpin only removes the entry; delete-shot deletes clips via `rows` and removes the entry in the same commit with no auto-restore. (4) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts`, clip creation via `rows` plus `clipIds` membership update.
tasks.executor_notes: The reverse switchToImages commit MUST be `type: 'rows'` — this is the only TimelineEditMutation variant carrying metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride together.

tasks.description: Commit the auto-sync preserve policy in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:231-453`. Preserve live timing of surviving members; appended images go to current group right edge; removed images delete only their clip with no trailing shift; reorder-only (membership unchanged, image order changes) is a documented intentional timeline no-op (add a code comment stating this rationale). Remove all `group.start` / shift-trailing-by-delta logic. Add tests for add/remove/reorder/no-op cases.

Render shot group rectangles from live clip positions only. Reduce `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts` to return `{ shotId, shotName, trackId, clipIds, color, mode }`. Compute `positionedShotGroups` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` as a pure function: resolve `clipIds` → live actions, sort by `at`, take first.start/last.end. Render empty group as a degraded placeholder — NEVER silently filter. Drop `startGroupEdgeResize`/`TimelineShotGroupResize` from state types (`useTimelineState.types.ts`), contexts (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`), `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and `TimelineEditor.tsx`.

Gate save and poll-sync on the shared interaction ref. In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.ts`: when `drag||resize`, stash latest pending payload; flush newest once on `clearSession`/`endResize`. In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePollSync.ts`: when `drag||resize`, defer poll/conflict reload; keep newest payload; re-evaluate after gesture ends. Remove temporary diagnostic `console.log` calls added during earlier debugging. Add regression tests for deferred-save flush and deferred-poll handling.

Update the broader test surface. (1) Confirm `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` is deleted (from T5). (2) DELETE OUTRIGHT two specific `useClipEditing.test.ts` test cases — do not field-strip: `:440-508` (`'restores image snapshots inline when a pinned video clip is deleted'`) — delete the entire `it(...)` block; and `:510-559` (`'projects pinned shot groups at the applyEdit commit boundary'`) — delete the entire `it(...)` block. Replacement coverage lives in T6 regression test, T7–T10 per-operation tests, and T15.4 cohesion sequence test. (3) Strip `start`/`children` assertions everywhere else in the test surface. Replace `type:'pinnedShotGroups'` timing assertions with `type:'rows'` (or `type:'config'` for the new-track case). (4) Add cohesion sequence test (pin → resize → drag → save → reload). (5) Add migration test from T2 (if not already present). (6) Add `switchToImages` regression test: video-mode group → call `switchToImages()` → assert image clips inserted at the video clip's previous `at` with cumulative offsets, video clip deleted, group's `clipIds`/`mode`/`videoAssetKey` updated, all in one commit, no `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` import. (7) Leave the known unrelated `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` assertion alone.

Reconcile features and run the full safety net. Manually verify shot label menu actions, per-clip context-menu lockdown, external shot drop, and BOTH switch-to-final-video and switch-to-images flows. Run in order: migration → save-utils → resize/drag/group → useTimelineCommit → useSwitchToFinalVideo → persistence/poll → full video-editor suite → `npx tsc --noEmit`. Compare net LOC across shot-group-related files (must decrease). Write a short throwaway reproduction script for the two reported symptoms (drag right edge of last clip in a group shrinks only that clip; auto-save does not interrupt a drag), run it to confirm the fix, then delete the script. If any test fails, fix the code and re-run until green. Do NOT create new test files for this — use the existing suite.

watch_items: Step 5 delete-list wording: `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is a LOCAL helper inside `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`, NOT an export of `pinned-group-projection.ts`. The real pinned-group-projection imports at useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:5-8 are `buildGroupFromChildren` and `replaceGroupChildren` (both already in the delete list). The local helper is removed as part of T11's inline rewrite of switchToImages.

T11 reverse `switchToImages()` commit MUST be `type: 'rows'` — it is the only TimelineEditMutation variant (per useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65) carrying metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride together.

DELIBERATE product change: the hidden reconciler-driven auto-restore (deleting a video-mode group's video clip silently reinserting image clips) is DROPPED. Empty groups render as a degraded placeholder. This applies ONLY to the hidden reconciler path, not to `switchToImages()`.
The explicit user-invocable `switchToImages()` feature is PRESERVED with a soft-tag rewrite. It must keep working end-to-end.
Same-track grouped drag must re-derive `clipIds` from post-`resolveOverlaps` `at` order and include a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` if the order changed. Do NOT hard-code 'no override needed'.
Track creation must stay at `type: 'config'` because `TimelineEditMutation.rows` has no `tracks` field (verified at useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65).
`pinnedShotGroupsOverride` typing must be tightened on BOTH `rows` and `config` variants to the soft-tag shape so geometry cannot leak back in.
`interactionStateRef` must be owned by `useTimelineState.ts` BEFORE `useTimelineSave` runs so the ref can be threaded through save/persistence/poll/drag/resize.
Auto-sync reorder-only case is an intentional timeline no-op — add a code comment; behavior differs from current, so be explicit.
DELETE (not field-strip) `useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508` and `:510-559` — both assert removed contracts.
Delete `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` outright; its `isInteractionActive` harness wires into removed helpers.
Remove temporary diagnostic `console.log` calls added during the buggy conversation phase.
Known pre-existing failing test `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` in TimelineCanvas test is unrelated — leave it alone per constraints.
Remotion preview must remain unchanged — clips[] is now the only source of truth; do not introduce new projection anywhere.
Migration is one-way: load → convert → save in new shape. Legacy `start`/`children` must never appear in the saved output after the refactor.
Scope is the video-editor tool only. No DB/schema/edge-function changes. No unrelated cleanups bundled.
`npx tsc --noEmit` must be clean. All video-editor tests must pass (minus the deleted cases and the known-unrelated failing case).
Net LOC across shot-group-related files MUST decrease.
Step 5 keeps a small `orderClipIdsByAt(clipIds, clips)` helper — use it consistently wherever membership is rebuilt.

sense_checks.question: Does `PinnedShotGroup` now contain ONLY `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, `mode`, `videoAssetKey`, `imageClipSnapshot`, with no `start`/`children` and a comment on `clipIds` ordering?

Does `repairConfig` detect legacy `start`+`children` groups and convert to soft-tag shape, and does the new migration test cover both load conversion and a round-trip save that emits no `start`/`children`?

Do `clonePinnedShotGroups`, `rowsToConfig`, and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` all emit soft-tag shape only, with no call to `projectPinnedShotGroups`? Were `render-bounds.validation.test.ts` fixtures updated?
Is `interactionStateRef` owned by `useTimelineState.ts` and threaded into save/persistence/poll/drag/resize hooks via arguments, with a single shared ref observed by all consumers?

Are all projection helpers deleted except `findEnclosingPinnedGroup`, `categorizeSelection`, and the new `orderClipIdsByAt` helper? Is `pinned-group-projection.test.ts` rewritten and `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` deleted?

Is `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` deleted entirely and `materializeData()` no longer calls it? Does the new regression test assert deleting a video-mode group's video clip leaves the group with empty `clipIds` and no auto-inserted images?

Do first-edge, last-edge, and interior-boundary resize operations emit `applyEdit({ type: 'rows' })` touching only the semantically correct clip(s), with the sequence test (resize → drag → resize) passing?
Is the `clip` resize session extended with `pairClipId`/`pairBoundary`, do the `group-edge`/`group-child-boundary` branches no longer exist, and is `onShotGroupEdgeResize` removed from the canvas?

Do same-track grouped drags emit `type:'rows'` with a soft-tag override when `resolveOverlaps` reorders, and do new-track grouped drags emit a single `type:'config'` with the new track plus soft-tag override? Is the `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` type tightened to soft-tag on both variants?
Do `moveClipToRow` and `createTrackAndMoveClip` route through the soft-tag model (rows vs config) with no remaining `moveGroup()` calls, and do keyboard/batch move callers still work?

Does `switchToImages()` emit a single `type:'rows'` commit anchored at the video clip's live `at` with cumulative offsets via `getClipDuration`, delete the video clip, update group via soft-tag override, and NOT import or define `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren`? Does `switchToFinalVideo()` also emit a single rows commit? Is `type:'pinnedShotGroups'` restricted to membership/metadata only?
Does `usePinnedShotGroups` auto-sync preserve live timing of surviving members, treat reorder-only as a documented no-op, and have no remaining `group.start` reads?

Does `useShotGroups` return the minimal soft-tag shape, is `positionedShotGroups` a pure function over live actions, do empty groups render as a degraded placeholder, and are `startGroupEdgeResize`/`TimelineShotGroupResize` removed from all context/type/provider sites?

Are save/persistence/poll all gated on the shared `interactionStateRef`, with newest-payload flush on gesture end? Are all diagnostic console.logs removed? Do deferred-save-flush and deferred-poll tests pass?

Are `useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508` and `:510-559` deleted outright (not field-stripped)? Are `start`/`children` assertions stripped elsewhere, and are cohesion-sequence, migration, and switchToImages regression tests added?

Does `npx tsc --noEmit` pass, does the full video-editor test suite pass (minus deleted/unrelated cases), has net LOC across shot-group files decreased, and did a throwaway repro script confirm both symptoms no longer reproduce before being deleted?

meta_commentary: Execution guidance: this is a structural rollback, not an incremental patch. Follow phase ordering strictly — types and migration first (T1-T2), then shared ref plumbing and helper deletion (T4-T5), then the reconciler deletion (T6), then rebuild edit operations as cohesion layer (T7-T11), then rendering and context cleanup (T12-T13), then save/poll gating (T14), then tests and verification (T15-T16). Key gotchas: (1) `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is a LOCAL helper in `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`, not a `pinned-group-projection.ts` export — the plan wording was slightly off but the effect is correct: it disappears as part of T11's inline rewrite once T5 deletes `buildGroupFromChildren`/`replaceGroupChildren`. (2) The reverse `switchToImages()` commit must explicitly be `type: 'rows'` — that is the only variant carrying metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride together (useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65). (3) The hidden auto-restore is intentionally dropped; the explicit `switchToImages()` is intentionally preserved — do not conflate them. (4) Track creation must stay `type: 'config'` because `rows` has no `tracks` field. (5) Same-track grouped drags must re-derive `clipIds` from post-`resolveOverlaps` `at` order — do not hard-code 'no override needed'. (6) `interactionStateRef` must be created in `useTimelineState.ts` and threaded via hook args; do not create it in `TimelineEditor.tsx` or `useTimelineSave` will not see it. (7) Delete (don't field-strip) the two named `useClipEditing.test.ts` cases. (8) Delete `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` outright. (9) Remove diagnostic console.logs added in the debugging session. (10) Scope is strictly the video-editor tool — no unrelated cleanups. (11) The pre-existing failing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` TimelineCanvas test is unrelated and should be left alone. Prefer Edit over Write throughout; do not create new docs. Net LOC across shot-group files must decrease — this is a must-achieve success criterion.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Reduce PinnedShotGroup to soft-tag fields
Step 2: Migrate legacy projection-shape configs in repairConfig + add migration test
Step 3: Strip projection from timeline-data / timeline-save-utils / render-bounds fixtures
Step 4: Create shared interactionStateRef in useTimelineState and thread it
Step 5: Delete projection-era helpers, keep lookups; rewrite/delete projection test files
Step 6: Delete reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows, drop hidden auto-restore, add delete-shot regression test
Step 7: Rebuild grouped resize as direct clip mutations
Step 8: Collapse group-edge/group-child-boundary sessions into clip session
Step 9: Rewrite grouped drag (rows/config branches) and simplify multi-drag-utils/resolve-overlaps
Step 11: Simplify group mutation entry points (commit, switchToFinalVideo, switchToImages, usePinnedShotGroups, useExternalDrop)
Step 12: Commit auto-sync preserve policy in usePinnedShotGroups
Step 13: Render shot group rectangles from live clips; drop startGroupEdgeResize from contexts/types/provider
Step 14: Gate save/persistence/poll on shared interaction ref; remove diagnostic logs
Step 15: Update broader test surface (delete named test cases, add new regression tests)
Step 16: Reconcile features and run full safety net (manual + automated + LOC compare)

validation.completeness_notes: All 16 plan steps map 1:1 to tasks T1–T16. T16 is the final verification task per requirements and covers manual feature checks, automated test runs, tsc, LOC comparison, and a throwaway repro script for the two reported symptoms.
